headline: Malaysia Cybersecurity Ranking Plunges From 8th to 48th
context: ITU Global Cybersecurity Index 2025 ranked Malaysia 48th down from 8th in 2020 reflecting relative decline.

cards.big: Malaysia cybersecurity ranking plunges from 8th in 2020 to 48th
cards.sub: Five-year decline reflects stagnation while others strengthened protections

cards.big: Malaysia scored 82 in 2020 but only 74 in 2025 as criteria tightened
cards.sub: Legal organizational and capacity building all scored below 2020

cards.big: Workforce gap of 12,000 professionals is largest contributor to decline
cards.sub: Private sector poaches government talent at 2-3 times salary

cards.big: Singapore maintained top-5 with RM2.8 billion annual cyber investment
cards.sub: Malaysia RM580 million is one-fifth of Singapore spending

cards.big: Ranking decline tests whether cybersecurity can be sustained without competitive funding
Cybersecurity ranking fell from 8th to 48th globally. Rankings change when investment stalls and threats evolve.
